steps.title: Check GCD of the coefficients
steps.content:
For an expression to factor a polynomial, it must divide every term evenly. The coefficients are 3, 57, and 15. Check if they share a common factor:
  • $3 = 3 \times 1$
  • $57 = 3 \times 19$
  • $15 = 3 \times 5$
All three are divisible by 3, so GCD = 3.

steps.title: Factor out 3
steps.content:
$$3x^2 + 57x + 15 = 3(x^2 + 19x + 5)$$
The factorization confirms that 3 divides all terms evenly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
